author: schmave
text:
```
b'\xff'.decode('utf-8')
```

In CPython, this raises UnicodeDecodeError, but in Brython, it returns the string `'ÿ'`. I think this error was introduced in commit [94a0657](https://github.com/brython-dev/brython/commit/94a065745b895734f39239bca0876b3e2ec8e2b6)

author: PierreQuentel
text: Improve error message when utf-8 decode fails. Related to issue [#1070](https://github.com/brython-dev/brython/issues/1070)

author: PierreQuentel
text: It's strange, I think I could reproduce the bug yesterday, but after making a minor change referenced above which doesn't change the algorithm, the code you mention actually raises an exception. Can you check again ?

author: schmave
text: Yes, I think that fixes it. What I saw was that the JS exception raised by `decodeURIComponent` wasn't being handled. I guess there's something else that is still causing the PR build to fail, though.
